name: The Benavente river vegas — Benavente
name_original: Las Vegas fluviales de Benavente — Benavente
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Santibáñez De Tera traverse a serene landscape defined by the Tera River and expansive green fields. The region, part of the Zamora province in Spain, offers a tranquil rural setting with low-traffic roads ideal for cycling. Cyclists can explore the picturesque riverside scenery and the broader Valle del Tera, which features diverse flora and fauna. The terrain generally consists of gentle gradients, with some routes extending towards areas like the Sierra de las Labradas for varied elevation. Best touring cycling routes around Santibáñez De Tera The most popular touring cycling route is Tera Valley Loop, a 35.0 miles (56.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views along the Tera River and through verdant fields. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop, a moderate 31.9 miles (51.3 km) path. It combines historical routes with varied rural landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the The Benavente river vegas — Benavente, a 39.1 miles (63.0 km) trail leading through river vegas and rural scenery, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Santibáñez De Tera is defined by the Tera River, green fields, and low-traffic rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Santibáñez De Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Santibáñez De Tera?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Santibáñez De Tera area. These routes offer a variety of experiences, from easy riverside rides to more challenging loops through varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Santibáñez De Tera?
A: The terrain around Santibáñez De Tera is generally characterized by gentle gradients, following the Tera River and winding through expansive green fields. While many routes offer peaceful, low-traffic rural roads, some extend towards areas like the Sierra de las Labradas, providing more varied elevation for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, Santibáñez De Tera offers several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. These routes typically follow the tranquil riverbanks and rural paths, providing a safe and enjoyable experience away from heavy traffic. For example, the region boasts a dedicated 25 km circular route designed with cyclist safety in mind, utilizing wide paths or bike lanes for 96% of its length.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes starting and ending in Santibáñez De Tera?
A: Absolutely. Many of the touring cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037068130/tera-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 56.3 km long and offers scenic views along the Tera River and through verdant fields.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while cycling?
A: Cyclists can combine their rides with visits to several historical and cultural sites. The village itself features traditional architecture and the Parish Church of San Juan Bautista. You can also visit the Roman Bridge over the Tera River. A significant highlight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, located on the Camino Sanabrés, known for its ancient sculpture of St. James the Apostle and unique equinox illumination.

Q: Are there any routes that incorporate historical paths like the Vía de la Plata?
A: Yes, you can explore routes that blend historical significance with natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037627997/via-de-la-plata-and-camino-del-sureste-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata and Camino del Sureste Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 51.3 km path that combines these historical routes with varied rural landscapes, offering a unique touring experience.

Q: What are the options for more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: For cyclists seeking a greater challenge, there are routes with more significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383988478/camarzana-de-tera-and-agavanzal-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is classified as difficult and covers 30.3 km, offering a more strenuous ride with rewarding views.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the routes?
A: The broader Valle del Tera is rich in flora and diverse animal life. While cycling, you might spot various birds, and there's a chance to see larger wildlife such as deer and wild boar, especially in the more secluded rural areas.

Q: Where can I find places to rest or have a picnic along the routes?
A: The region offers several spots for rest and relaxation. You can find a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3858806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break. Additionally, the sports area known as 'La Playa' near the town center provides amenities and a pleasant environment by the water.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Santibáñez De Tera?
A: The touring cycling routes around Santibáñez De Tera are well-regarded by the komoot community, with over 20 touring cyclists having explored the varied terrain. Riders often praise the serene environment, the picturesque riverside scenery, and the peaceful, low-traffic rural roads that make for an enjoyable cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the wider Benavente area?
A: Yes, some routes extend beyond Santibáñez De Tera into the broader Benavente region. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383275431/the-benavente-river-vegas-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Benavente river vegas — Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, a 63.0 km trail that leads through river vegas and rural scenery, offering a longer exploration of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Santibáñez De Tera?
A: The tranquil environment of Santibáñez De Tera, with its green fields and riverside paths, is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the Tera River where the natural surroundings provide a refreshing backdrop.
tips: Ruins of a palace and castle built in the 15th century by members of the Escobar family, who were the lords of the town. Of the Palace, now in a state of abandonment, the outer masonry walls have been preserved. The castle is visible from the road.
Brime de Urz is a small town south of the Sierra De Carpurias. The most notable monument in the city is the Ermita de San Esteban, which stands on top of a hill overlooking the city.
A small town in the northeast of Zamora near the Sierra de Carpurias and the Eria river.

Benavente is a crossroads of both roads and rivers. Its settlement has given shelter to pilgrims, troops and even Napoleon who, on his way to Astorga, lodged his troops leaving an unforgettable trail of destruction. It also gives way to the fields of Zamora, leaving León behind. Its monumental buildings testify to the importance of its location in the passage of history. It is an ideal place, with all the services, to make a stop along the way.
In the historic center, the Church of Santa María del Azogue, from the 16th century, and in Gothic style, stands out as the main monument of Benavente.

Castle-fortress from the 12th century located on top of a hill overlooking the surrounding landscape. Since 1972 it has been owned by the Parador Nacional de Turismo group and functions as a vacation hotel.

Mózar is a small town located at the confluence of the Tera and Órbigo rivers in the Benavente valley. Famous for its natural activities, there are several campsites near the river beaches for bathing and abundant places for trout fishing.
Bridge over the Tera river between Sitrama and Santibáñez de Tera. The river is a tributary of the Esla river, a natural park of special conservation.
